title: papaver dubium var. stipitatum
intro: Papaver dubium var. stipitatum, also known as the Himalayan poppy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Himalayan poppy include reducing pain and inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and improving sleep quality. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts have been used in various herbal preparations, including ointments and salves, to support overall well-being.
